AbortMultipartUpload.name: AbortMultipartUpload
AbortMultipartUpload.documentation: Cancels an in-progress multipart upload for the specified object in the specified bucket. After cancellation, no additional parts can be uploaded using that upload ID and storage consumed by previously uploaded parts will be released.

CompleteMultipartUpload.name: CompleteMultipartUpload
CompleteMultipartUpload.documentation:
Finalizes a multipart upload by assembling all previously uploaded parts into a single object. The request body must include the list of parts with their part numbers and ETag values in ascending order (lowest part number to highest part number).

The maximum size limit of a multipart object is 16 TiB. This limit is different from the standard S3 API limit of 48.8 TiB.

User-defined object metadata is supported for the CompleteMultipartUpload APIs and is limited to 2 KB in size.

User-provided metadata header names are stored in lowercase.

CopyObject.name: CopyObject
CopyObject.documentation:
Creates a server-side copy of an existing object within the same bucket or across buckets. The source object is identified through the x-amz-copy-source header.

User-defined object metadata is supported for the CopyObject APIs and is limited to 2 KB in size.

User-provided metadata header names are stored in lowercase.

CreateMultipartUpload.name: CreateMultipartUpload
CreateMultipartUpload.documentation: Initiates a multipart upload and returns an upload ID. Use this upload ID in subsequent UploadPart and CompleteMultipartUpload requests. Nutanix Objects supports up to 10,000 parts per multipart upload.

PutObjectAcl.name: PutObjectAcl

PutObjectLegalHold.name: PutObjectLegalHold
PutObjectLegalHold.documentation: Applies or removes an Object Lock legal hold on the specified object. The bucket must have Object Lock configured. For details on legal hold, refer here.

PutObjectLockConfiguration.name: PutObjectLockConfiguration
PutObjectLockConfiguration.documentation:
Sets the Object Lock configuration for the specified bucket to enable WORM protection. For more details on WORM, refer here.

PutObjectRetention.name: PutObjectRetention
PutObjectRetention.documentation:
Sets the retention configuration for the specified object to enable WORM protection. For more details on WORM, refer here.

PutObjectTagging.name: PutObjectTagging
PutObjectTagging.documentation: Applies a tag set to the specified object. More details on object tagging can be found here.

PutPublicAccessBlock.name: PutPublicAccessBlock

SelectObjectContent.documentation:
Runs a structured query (SQL) against the contents of the specified object and returns matching records. More details on S3 Select can be found here.

Nutanix Objects only supports CSV as both input and output serialization formats.

Only the fields mentioned below are supported.

UploadPart.name: UploadPart
UploadPart.documentation: Uploads a single part in a multipart upload. The multipart upload must initiated using CreateMultipartUpload API before uploading any parts. Each request must specify the upload ID and a sequential part number between 1 and 10,000.

UploadPartCopy.name: UploadPartCopy
UploadPartCopy.documentation: Uploads a part in a multipart upload by copying data from given source object and byte range.

WriteGetObjectResponse.name: WriteGetObjectResponse
WriteGetObjectResponse.documentation: Nutanix Objects does not support this operation.

GetObjectsAllDomains.name: GetObjectsAllDomains
GetObjectsAllDomains.documentation: Returns all domain endpoints configured for the Nutanix Objects deployment.

PutBucketNfsConfiguration.name: PutBucketNfsConfiguartion
PutBucketNfsConfiguration.documentation: Sets the NFS access configuration for the specified bucket. NFS-configured buckets support both S3 and NFS protocol access. More details on this feature can be found here.

GetBucketNfsConfiguration.name: GetBucketNfsConfiguration
GetBucketNfsConfiguration.documentation: Retrieves the NFS access configuration of the specified bucket.

PutBucketInventoryStatus.name: PutBucketInventoryStatus
PutBucketInventoryStatus.documentation:
This operation enables you to update the status of multiple inventory configurations using a single HTTP request. If you know the bucket inventory configurations that you want to update, this operation provides a suitable alternative to sending individual update requests, reducing per-request overhead.

The request contains a list of up to 1000 inventory configuration keys that you want to update. In the XML, you provide the bucket names and respective inventory config ids. For each bucket name-inventory config id pair, it performs an update operation and returns the result of that update, success, or failure, in the response.

The operation supports two modes for the response: verbose and quiet. By default, the operation uses verbose mode in which the response includes the result of deletion of each key in your request. In quiet mode the response includes only keys where the update operation encountered an error. For a successful update, the operation does not return any information about the update in the response body.

GetBucketInventoryStatus.name: GetBucketInventoryStatus
GetBucketInventoryStatus.documentation:
This operation enables you to read the status of multiple inventory configurations using a single HTTP request. If you know the bucket inventory configurations that you want to get the status of, this operation provides a suitable alternative to sending individual get status requests, reducing per-request overhead.

The request contains a list of up to 1000 inventory configuration keys that you want to get the status of. In the XML, you provide the bucket names and respective inventory config ids. For each bucket name-inventory config id pair, it performs a get status operation and returns the result of that update, success, or failure, in the response.

ListFederationMembers.name: ListFederationMembers

IfMatch.documentation: Performs the operation only if the ETag matches the specified value.

IfNoneMatch.documentation:
Returns the object only if the ETag does not match the specified value.

Nutanix Objects validates the If-Match and If-None-Match condition only at the start and end of an object upload. If it matches at both points, the request is accepted. Even if multiple competing requests modify the object state during the upload and cancel effects of each other, the conditional request is still considered valid and accepted.
